programme.title: Power & S3 - "MMA - Nick Hein"
programme.desc: Nick Hein is a professional Mixed Martial Artist from Cologne and signed with the UFC. His ultimate goal is to become MMA world champion.

programme.title: Power & S3 - "Extreme Martial Arts – Shaina West"
programme.desc: Shaina West taught herself martial arts by watching Youtube recovering from a motorcycle accident. The videos she posted went viral and ‘The Samurider’ was born.

programme.title: Power & S3 - "Karate – James Pankiewicz"
programme.desc: Englishman and karateka James Pankiewicz came to Okinawa, the birthplace of Karate, for his wife and to open a Dojo and the immensely popular Dojo Bar.

programme.title: Breakthrough S1 - "Coronavirus: Don't Blame Bats"
programme.desc: Bats are getting their share of the blame for the COVID-19 pandemic, but are they really at fault?

programme.title: Breakthrough S1 - "Peru's Mysterious Cloud Warriors"
programme.desc: Archeologists and geneticists are uncovering surprising details about one of the most mysterious civilizatiions in Pre-Incan South America.

programme.title: Breakthrough S1 - "Decoding Megalodon"
programme.desc: Scientists have concluded that "meg" was able to achieve its gargantuan proportions by starting its carnivorous diet early -- in the womb.

programme.title: Breakthrough S1 - "Noah's Ark for the Humans"
programme.desc: Scientists believe that within a few decades many useful microbes will have disappeared so they want to create a vault for Human Microbiota.

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Hallucinogens"
programme.desc: While psychedelics were originally seen as a tool for spiritual enlightenment and healing, new research and studies are revisiting psychedelics to reveal their potential clinical and therapeutic impact.

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Music"
programme.desc: We use music to nurture our children, comfort the sick, and worship the unseen. Music is inherently a human act, but today, artificial intelligence and algorithms have gotten more and more involved in music production, distribution, and marketing.

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Communication"
programme.desc: From the first written word to the Internet, we’ve continued to innovate how we communicate with each other. What happens when technology advances to the point that ideas and thoughts can be shared instantly?

programme.title: Past, Present, Future S1 - "...of Transportation"
programme.desc: From empires to major metropolitan cities, transportation has been crucial. As cities grow more congested and overwhelmed with toxic air, how will future transportation help get us where we need to be?

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment S1 - "CUBICLE"
programme.desc: A futuristic designer, Robert Propst, dreamed up “The Action Office” as a workplace utopia that would unleash the modern knowledge worker’s vast untapped potential.

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment S1 - "BARCODE"
programme.desc: Barcodes have radically changed global commerce, helping fuel the rise of everything from massive companies like Walmart to world powers like China. And it all started with a man daydreaming on a beach in 1949.

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment S1 - "FLORIDA"
programme.desc: Florida wasn’t always the Sunshine State. In the 1920s, an army of real estate boosters set out to redefine Florida from an economic backwater to a vacation destination, sparking a land boom–and bust–the likes of which America had never seen before.

programme.title: The Lightbulb Moment S1 - "MAUVE"
programme.desc: In 1856, a chemistry student accidentally created a strange substance with a rich purple hue -- the world’s first synthetic dye. In just a few years, ‘Mauve’ would take over the fashion industry and enable stunning advances in medicine.

programme.title: Strip The City S2 - "Machu Picchu"
programme.desc: Stripping away the granite, CGI reveals the quake-proof walls and hidden canals that allowed Machu Picchu to survive at 11,000 feet.

programme.title: Strip The City S2 - "Los Angeles"
programme.desc: Stripping Los Angeles to its core, CGI reveals the hidden steel and fault lines that help this earthquake-prone city survive "the Big One."

programme.title: Strip The City S2 - "New Orleans"
programme.desc: CGI peels back the Superdome and the city’s silt to reveal the massive pumps and barriers keeping New Orleans from sinking.

programme.title: Strip The City S2 - "New York"
programme.desc: Dissecting New York's stone and steel, CGI reveals the inflatable subway plugs and bedrock foundations shielding the city from Atlantic superstorms.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet S5 - "Sports & The Environment"
programme.desc: In this episode, we travel to the intersection of sports and our environment.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet S5 - "Student Explorer"
programme.desc: In this episode, we reveal the winner of our second ever #StudentExplorer Contest.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet S5 - "Students Taking The Lead"
programme.desc: In this episode, we learn from the students who want to change the world.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet S5 - "Threatened Places"
programme.desc: In this episode, we visit a few examples of threatened places.

programme.title: Angry Planet S1 - "Fin del Mundo"
programme.desc: Some of the fiercest weather on the planet can be experienced at the southern tip of Argentina and Chile– the area the South Americans call the “Fin del Mundo” – the end of the world.

programme.title: Angry Planet S1 - "Boiling Lake"
programme.desc: Dominica, the most volcanic and mountainous island in the Caribbean, is home to the Boiling Lake, a large waterfall-fed lake of furiously boiling water. Adventurer George Kourounis becomes the first person ever to traverse across the lake on a rope.

programme.title: Angry Planet S1 - "Midwest Stormfest"
programme.desc: George Kourounis returns to “Tornado Alley” with three of his fellow stormchasers to see what the 2007 Storm Season will bring.

programme.title: Angry Planet S2 - "Wild Water Two"
programme.desc: One of the most exciting episodes of season one is “Wild Water” – our look at Great White Sharks, water-filled caves and high voltage marine action.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S3 - "D-Day By Air"
programme.desc: Terry goes back to paratrooper school and will see how difficult it was for soldiers engaging the enemy while parachuting down to safety on D-Day. He will attempt with 3 different weapons to hit several targets while descending from the air.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S3 - "Saving Private Terry"
programme.desc: Terry gets to relive part of his military carrier by attempting the famous sniper shot from Saving Private Ryan, and Larry and Terry demonstrate how sniper technology has developed over the years and test some amazing sniper shots.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction? S4 - "The Last Crusade of Indiana Terry"
programme.sub-title: The Last Crusade of Indiana Terry
programme.desc: Terry channels his inner Dr. Jones and tests 1 scene from Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade containing 3 different tests, two of which they will need a tank for. The duo will travel down to Uvalde, TX to visit Drivetanks.com to see if a tank round will topple over a car, and if a 9mm round can travel through three Germans.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet S5 - "Hidden Nature"
programme.desc: In this episode, we see a few hidden parts of nature.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet S5 - "Research in Remote Locations"
programme.desc: In this episode, we visit a few remote areas to see how research and restoration is conducted.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet S5 - "Science Communicators"
programme.desc: In this episode, we’ll meet a few different science communicators

programme.title: Angry Planet S2 - "Hottest & Coldest"
programme.desc: George experiences the most extreme climates in North America. First he visits the hottest place on the continent – Death Valley, then the coldest – Snag, Yukon.

programme.title: Angry Planet S2 - "Hurricanes '07"
programme.desc: We hope Season Two will give us the opportunity to make the quintessential Angry Planet show – a full episode exploring a major hurricane that makes landfall in Florida or the Carolinas.

programme.title: Angry Planet S2 - "B.C. Caving"
programme.desc: In the porous limestone of Canada’s West Coast, great forces are at work under the ground, building and eroding the underground into fantastic shapes. In this episode we’ll witness George’s explorations of these massive underground cave systems.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "Spirit of Discovery"
programme.desc: Dive in and check some ingenious inventions that have changed our daily lives.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "Porsche Sound Nacht"
programme.desc: Go on a journey through the sounds of the most iconic Porsche engines.

programme.title: Skuld: A Look into the Future S1 - "Energy Globe Award 21st - Part 1"
programme.desc: Watch the first part of a feature on the second award ceremony for sustainability projects that can change the world.

programme.title: Xploration Earth 2050 S2 - "Nature Inspires The Future"
programme.desc: Host Chuck Pell sees how humpback whales are showing designers how to save millions in energy costs.

programme.title: Xploration Earth 2050 S2 - "The Future Of Warfare"
programme.desc: Join host Chuck Pell as he plays videos games that are shaping modern battle plans.

programme.title: Xploration Earth 2050 S2 - "Humans Adapt To The Future"
programme.desc: Host Chuck Pell tries out a underwater ‘tent’ that allows explorers to spend hours, and even days, beneath the ocean’s surface.

programme.title: Xploration Outer Space S2 - "Space Disasters"
programme.desc: Join host Emily Calandrelli as she explores the most prominent disasters in our journey through the cosmos, and what we learned from each set-back.

programme.title: Xploration Outer Space S2 - "Why We Explore"
programme.desc: Host Emily Calandrelli makes the case for space exploration.

programme.title: Xploration Outer Space S2 - "Exoplanets"
programme.desc: Join host Emily Calandrelli as she explores the fascinating world of exoplanets.

programme.title: Sci Q S1 - "Who’s the Smartest in the Room?"
programme.sub-title: Who’s the Smartest in the Room?
programme.desc: Fast-paced, pop-science show that will test your science I.Q. Where’s the hottest place in North America? Does a camel’s hump store water or fat? Our planet is moving through space at 67,000 mph, so why don’t we feel it moving?

programme.title: Sci Q S1 - "Dare to be Smart"
programme.desc: Fast-paced, pop-science show that will test your science I.Q. When was the first virtual-reality headset created? What is hydroponic farming? What’s the difference between a grizzly bear and a brown bear?

programme.title: Sci Q S1 - "Sci Smarts"
programme.desc: Fast-paced, pop-science show that will test your science I.Q. What’s the name of the largest fault line in California? How does the Venus Flytrap catch bugs? Is the far side of the moon always dark?

programme.title: Quick Connections with Alie Ward S1 - "Faking Faces"
programme.desc: Alie Ward falls through the wormholes of history into the Louisiana swamps, then journeys on to discover how poker-faced mischief and a mathematical code for winning games have brought us to machines that can invent very real faces.

programme.title: Xploration DIY Sci S5 - "The Higgins Show"
programme.desc: After years of feeling like he’s been the butt of all of Steve Spangler’s jokes, his sidekick Higginsworth threatens to leave unless Steve quits his pranks.

programme.title: Xploration DIY Sci S5 - "Science Fair"
programme.desc: Steve Spangler’s sidekick Higginsworth panics when he has to deliver a science fair project.

programme.title: Xploration DIY Sci S5 - "Museum Of Science, Boston #2"
programme.desc: Host Steve Spangler and his sidekick Higginsworth return to the Museum of Science, Boston, where they take simple demonstrations they’ve done on DIY Sci and make them gigantic!
